title: ReleaseKit Configuration

push.description: Whether to push changes to remote

httpsTokenEnv.description: Environment variable name containing a GitHub token for HTTPS pushes

tagTemplate.description: Template for Git tags. Available variables: ${version} (version number), ${prefix} (versionPrefix value, e.g. 'v'), ${packageName} (sanitized package name, e.g. 'scope-pkg'). Example: "${packageName}-${prefix}${version}" produces "scope-pkg-v1.2.3".

baselineTagTemplate.description: Optional secondary tag template for an internal 'baseline' marker that records the release commit on the source branch. Use this when tagTemplate resolves to a tag that gets force-moved off the source branch by a downstream step (e.g. a GitHub Action distributing built artifacts at the version tag) — the baseline tag stays on the release commit so future version-bump and changelog calculations can still find the previous release. Must contain a ${version} placeholder so the baseline prefix can be derived. Supports the same variables as tagTemplate. Example: "release/${prefix}${version}" produces "release/v1.2.3".

sync.description: Global lockstep versioning. true is sugar for one implicit fixed group of every package — it shares the same mechanism as version.groups. Set to false when using version.groups; sync: true alongside groups is treated as the implicit all-packages fixed group taking precedence (a config conflict, warned about at runtime).

packages.description: Package patterns (exact names, @scope/*, or globs) whose matched packages form this group. Same matching rules as version.packages.

sync.description: fixed: all members release together at the shared group version. linked: only changed members release, all at the same computed version. independent: only changed members release, each on its own commit-driven version line (no shared version), but the set ships atomically.

groups.description: Named version groups. Each group binds a set of package patterns to a fixed, linked, or independent sync mode. fixed: any releasable change in any member releases ALL members at the shared group version (bump(max(member baselines))). linked: only members with releasable changes release, but every releasing member shares the same computed version. independent: only members with releasable changes release, each on its own commit-driven version line (no shared version), but the set is atomic — targeting any member pulls in the whole group. Packages not matched by any group version independently. A package may belong to at most one group. Set version.sync to false when using groups.

sharedPackages.description: Foundational packages whose changes belong in every package’s changelog. A commit touching only a shared package (exact name or glob) is classified as repo-level and surfaced under "Project-wide changes" rather than attributed to that one package. Default: none — no package is treated as shared unless declared.

mainPackage.description: Package to use for version determination

pattern.description: Glob or regex matched against the branch name (e.g. 'release/*')

releaseType.description: Version bump type applied when this pattern matches

branchPatterns.description: Branch name patterns for version determination

defaultReleaseType.description: Default release type when no pattern matches

zeroMajor.description: Pre-1.0 handling of commit-inferred breaking changes. 'spec' (default): bump the 0.x minor (0.24.0 → 0.25.0), per semver §4. 'strict': bump the next major (→ 1.0.0). Inferred path only — explicit overrides (--bump major, bump:major) always graduate to 1.0.0.

paths.description: Directories to search for Cargo.toml files
Directories to search for pubspec.yaml files

push.description: Push tags and commits to remote

httpsTokenEnv.description: Environment variable name containing a GitHub token for HTTPS pushes

copyFiles.description: Files to copy to package before publishing

publishOrder.description: Explicit publish order for npm packages; empty auto-sorts dependencies first
Order in which to publish packages
Order in which to publish packages

body.description: Source for GitHub release body. 'auto': use release notes if enabled, else changelog, else GitHub auto. 'releaseNotes': use LLM-generated release notes. 'changelog': use changelog entries. 'generated': GitHub auto-generated. 'none': no body.

titleTemplate.description: Template for the GitHub release title when a package name is resolved. Available variables: ${packageName} (original scoped name, e.g. '@scope/pkg'), ${version} (e.g. 'v1.0.0'). Version-only tags always use the tag string directly.

skipPackages.description: Package names to exclude from GitHub release creation

oneOf.description: Set to false to disable changelog generation

mode.description: Where to write changelog files. root: repo root only. packages: per-package (monorepos). both: repo root and per-package. When omitted entirely (no changelog config), defaults to root.

oneOf.description: Set to false to disable release notes

dir.description: Directory for the per-version release-notes files.

file.description: Optional in-repo file output. Omit to keep release notes only on the GitHub release body (the default). When set, writes one immutable Markdown file per version under `dir` — release-notes/<package>/<version>.md in a monorepo, release-notes/<version>.md in a single-package repo — giving a browsable, provider-independent per-release history.

templates.description: Template for rendering release notes (e.g. to add docs-site frontmatter). Takes precedence over LLM prose and the default formatted section.

default.name: Breaking
default.description: Breaking changes that require user action to upgrade

default.name: Developer
default.description: Internal changes: CI, tooling, dependencies, refactoring

name.description: Category label shown in release notes (e.g. 'Features')

description.description: LLM instruction describing what commits belong in this category

scopes.description: Conventional commit scopes assigned to this category

mode.description: Scope allowlist source: 'restricted' uses rules.allowed, 'packages' derives scopes from workspace package names, 'none' strips all scopes, 'unrestricted' allows any scope

allowed.description: Explicit list of valid scope names; commits with unlisted scopes trigger invalidScopeAction

invalidScopeAction.description: Action for commits whose scope is not in the allowed list: 'remove' strips the scope, 'keep' leaves it, 'fallback' substitutes fallbackScope

enhance.description: Instruction override for the enhance task

categorize.description: Instruction override for the categorize task

enhanceAndCategorize.description: Instruction override for the combined enhance + categorize task

summarize.description: Instruction override for the summarize task

releaseNotes.description: Instruction override for the release-notes task

instructions.description: Per-task instruction overrides appended to the built-in prompts.

examples.description: Number of few-shot examples to include in LLM prompts (0–5).

pullRequests.description: Include linked pull request titles/bodies as context.

context.description: Additional context sources for the LLM.

categoryOrder.description: Explicit ordering of categories in the output. Categories not listed retain their configured order after the listed ones.

cache.description: Cache LLM responses on disk (under the OS temp dir), keyed by a hash of the provider, model, prompt, and request options. A re-run or backfill with the same inputs reuses the cached generation instead of re-calling the provider. Off by default.

items.description: Static list of links to append.

fromPRBodyMarker.description: Marker string in PR bodies; content after it is extracted and appended as links.

links.description: Extra links to append to the release notes.

oneOf.description: Set to false to disable the first-release placeholder intro.

text.description: Placeholder intro line for a package's first release. Supports ${packageName} and ${version}. Defaults to a factual line so it reads cleanly even when published unedited.

firstRelease.description: First-release placeholder intro, shown when a package has no prior version (previousVersion is null). Default-on with a factual line; set to false to disable.

updateStrategy.description: How to update existing changelog files. 'prepend' adds new entries to the top; 'regenerate' rewrites the file from scratch.

releaseStrategy.description: How releases are delivered. 'direct': release on merge to main. 'manual': releases triggered manually (e.g. workflow_dispatch). 'standing-pr': changes accumulate in a release PR; gate mode acts as the immediate-release evaluator, firing only for merges labelled with the immediate label.

releaseTrigger.description: What triggers a release. 'label': a PR bump label (bump:patch/minor/major) is required. 'commit': conventional commits drive the bump automatically; every merge can trigger a release.

prPreview.description: Enable PR preview comments showing what would be released if the PR is merged. Set to false to disable.

autoRelease.description: Automatically trigger a release when CI conditions are met, without manual intervention.

skipPatterns.description: Commit message prefixes that suppress a release. The default matches the release commit template to prevent release loops.

minChanges.description: Minimum number of packages with releasable changes required to trigger a release.

stable.description: Label to graduate a prerelease to stable

skip.description: Label to suppress a release on this PR

immediate.description: Label to bypass the standing PR for one merge — triggers a direct release. Standing-pr mode only.

retry.description: Label to retry a failed publish by re-applying it to a merged standing PR. Standing-pr mode only.

previewNotes.description: Label on the standing PR that generates LLM release notes on demand into an editable region in the PR body, for review and editing before merge. Standing-pr mode only.

major.description: Label to force a major bump

minor.description: Label to force a minor bump

patch.description: Label to force a patch bump

withPrerequisites.description: Label on the standing PR that also releases the changed prerequisites (transitive internal dependencies) of the targeted/scoped packages — each at its own commit-driven bump. Standing-pr mode only.

labels.description: PR label names used for release control. Override to match your repository's label conventions.

scopeLabels.description: Map of scope labels to package patterns. When a PR has a label matching a key, only packages matching the corresponding pattern are released.

branch.description: Branch name for the standing release PR.

title.description: PR title template. Variables: ${count} (publishable package count), ${version} (raw version), ${tag} (version with tag prefix). Must start with 'chore: release' to match the default skip pattern on squash merge. Default depends on the versioning strategy: 'chore: release ${tag}' in sync mode, 'chore: release ${count} package(s)' otherwise.

labels.description: Labels to apply to the standing release PR.

deleteBranchOnMerge.description: Whether to auto-delete the release branch after the PR is merged.

mergeMethod.description: Merge method to use when merging the standing release PR via CLI.

minAge.description: Minimum age of the standing PR before it can be merged. Duration string, e.g. '6h', '30m', '1d'. Gate enforced via the releasekit/standing-pr commit status check; configure it as a required status check in branch protection to block merges.

minPackages.description: Minimum number of packages with releasable changes required to create or maintain the standing PR. Below this threshold the PR is closed and no new PR is opened.

requiredPermission.description: Minimum repository permission an actor needs to steer the standing PR — tick/untick selection checkboxes, apply release labels, and (with branch protection) merge. Default: admin.

allowedActors.description: Extra actors authorized regardless of permission level: GitHub usernames, or "@org/team-slug" to authorize a whole team. Team-membership checks need a token with org read:org scope (a PAT or GitHub App), not the default GITHUB_TOKEN; without it, team entries resolve to "not a member".

enforceMergeAuthor.description: Refuse to publish the standing PR when the actor who merged it is not authorized (defense-in-depth behind a branch-protection ruleset, which is the primary merge gate). Set false to rely on branch protection alone.

authorization.description: Restrict who can steer the standing PR — its selection checkboxes, release labels, and merge. Omit to allow anyone with the GitHub permission GitHub itself requires for each action (today’s behavior).

standingPr.description: Configuration for the standing release PR feature (ci.releaseStrategy: 'standing-pr').

ci.description: CI automation configuration for release triggers, PR previews, and label management

steps.description: Which steps to run by default. Omitting a step is equivalent to --skip-<step>.

skipPatterns.description: Commit message prefixes that prevent a release (e.g. 'chore(deps):', 'ci:')

minChanges.description: Minimum number of packages with releasable changes required to trigger a release

githubRelease.description: Set to false to disable GitHub release creation in CI

notes.description: Set to false to disable changelog generation in CI

description: Configuration schema for ReleaseKit - Automated versioning, changelog generation, and publishing
